description_long: This object is a large, historical-style cannon mounted on a wooden carriage with four wheels. The cannon features a long, cylindrical barrel with decorative rings and a slightly flared muzzle, typical of artillery pieces from the 17th to 19th centuries. The carriage is robust, constructed with thick wooden planks and reinforced with metal fittings, and includes two large side wheels and two smaller front wheels for mobility. Metal trunnions secure the barrel to the carriage, allowing for vertical adjustment of the firing angle. The rear of the cannon includes a knob-like cascabel, and the overall construction suggests it was designed for stationary use in defensive positions or on fortifications. The surface appears to be metallic for the barrel and wooden for the carriage, with a uniform, unpainted finish.
description: A large, historical cannon with a long metal barrel mounted on a sturdy wooden wheeled carriage, featuring decorative rings and robust construction.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular muzzle opening and the symmetrical arrangement of the wheels and carriage.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the barrel, the carriage structure, and the wheel arrangement.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the cascabel at the end of the barrel and the back of the carriage.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the barrel and carriage from the other side.
